title: Seeqov Thranx
content:

{{Quote|She's become a master of something alien to the hive-minded creatures—deception. Unlike most idealists, she was able to see the situation from all sides.|The Bloodletter describes Thranx|<ref name="BactaBasics2" />}}
'''Seeqov Thranx''' was a [[Vratix/Legends|Vratix]] of the [[Seeqov hive-clan|Seeqov]] [[hive-clan]] who identified more as [[Gender/Legends|female]] than male. She was a field inspector and research [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]]. Her most notable career accomplishment, for which she was famous on all over [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]], was helping eradicate a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Fungus/Legends|fungus]] epidemic which threatened the [[Alazhi/Legends|alazhi]] harvest in [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra's]] southern hemisphere. She later became involved with the [[Ashern]], a Vratix independent movement. When the Ashern hired a corporate [[Spy/Legends|spy]] known as the [[Bloodletter]] to infiltrate the [[Bacta Cartel/Legends|Bacta Cartel]], they appointed Thranx as the Bloodletter's partner. After she revealed that the Ashern had been growing [[kolcta]], a highly effective hybrid between [[Kolto/Legends|kolto]] and [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]], the Bloodletter killed her and took the secret to their true master on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

==Biography==
Seeqov Thranx worked for many years as a field inspector and research [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] on [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]]. She was instrumental in eradicating a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] fungal epidemic that had threatened a significant harvest of [[Alazhi/Legends|alazhi]]. Shortly after this, she was recruited into the Vratix independence movement known as the [[Ashern]]. In this capacity, she was the primary contact for their agent known as the [[Bloodletter]].<ref name="BactaBasics2">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031113thyferra2|series=Thyferra: Bacta Basics|text=Part 2: Ashern to Ashern}}</ref> The Bloodletter was a freelance corporate spy who had been hired by the Ashern to infiltrate the [[Bacta Cartel/Legends|Bacta Cartel]].<ref name="BactaBasics1">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031106thyferra1|series=Thyferra: Bacta Basics|text=Part 1: The Thriving Season}}</ref> and when they struggled to find a way to infiltrate both [[Xucphra Corporation]] and [[Zaltin Corporation]], Thranx hit upon the solution of presenting them as a field inspector.<ref name="BactaBasics2" />

After she had been working with the Bloodletter for a year, the pair were ambushed in an alazhi field by a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior in blue and silver [[Armor/Legends|armor]]. The warrior shot both Thranx and the Bloodletter, believing them both to be dead.<ref name="BactaBasics3">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031120thyferra3|series=Thyferra: Bacta Basics|text=Part 3: Fields of Dreams}}</ref> Thranx was mortally wounded but was able to produce [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] within her own body to heal her companion—a particularly effective form of bacta known as [[kolcta]], a hybrid with [[Kolto/Legends|kolto]]. The Ashern had been growing it in order to be able to break the monopolies held by the two bacta corporations. Thranx revealed this secret to the Bloodletter so that they could pass it on to the other Ashern before she died. However, the Bloodletter, who was a double agent, killed Thranx and instead passed the secret onto their employer on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="BactaBasics4">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031126thyferra4|series=Thyferra: Bacta Basics|text=Part 4: The Kolcta Generation}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Like many Vratix who worked in frequent contact with [[Human/Legends|humans]], Thranx identified more strongly with one binary [[Gender/Legends|gender]] than another and permitted the use of "she" as a pronoun, a status referred to as "female-oriented."<ref name="BactaBasics2" /> Thranx used either the first-person pronoun "we," standard for Vratix, or "Seeqov" to refer to herself.<ref name="BactaBasics4" /> The Bloodletter observed that while Thranx was an idealist, she was able to see all sides of a matter.<ref name="BactaBasics2" />

==Skills and abilities==
Seeqov Thranx was an accomplished research scientist in the field of bacta production. She was also fluent in a number of languages, such as [[Arkanian (language)/Legends|Arkanian]], [[Ithorese/Legends|Ithorese]], [[Kubazian/Legends|Kubazi]], [[Lepese]], and [[Twi'leki|Ryl]]. During her time as a rebel, she became skilled at deception, a skill she also applied in [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] matches.<ref name="BactaBasics2" />

==Equipment==
Thranx possessed a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], field [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratory]] kit, [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]], and a [[Vibrodagger/Legends|vibrodagger]]. She also maintained a private [[Seeqov Thranx's apartment|apartment]] in [[Xozhixi]].<ref name="BactaBasics2" />
